perturbation.original_text: The Recipient shall be responsible for any improper use of the Confidential Information by its Representatives and agrees, at its own expense, to take all reasonable measures, i ncluding but not limited to court proceedings, to restrain its Representatives from unauthorized disclosure or use of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient shall only be responsible for any willful improper use of the Confidential Information by its Representatives and agrees, at its own expense, to take all reasonable measures, i ncluding but not limited to court proceedings, to restrain its Representatives from unauthorized disclosure or use of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation adds 'only be responsible for any willful' to the definition of Recipient Responsibilities for Representative improper use. This new term constrasts with the previous version of the definition. If the improper use wasn't willful, it could cause a dispute regarding the Responsibilities of the Recipient to what was agreed upon.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Use of Confidential Information . As a condition to the disclosure of the Confidential Information, the Recipient agrees to keep the Confidential Information confidential and not to disclose or otherwise use the Confi dential Information for any purpose, other than for its business relationship with PHCS, without the prior written consent of PHCS. Accordingly, the Recipient agrees to treat the C onfidential Information which it receives as it would its own confidential information and to take all reasonable precautions to prevent the unauthorized disclosure to any third party of any Confidential Information. The Recipient further agrees to disclose the Con fidential Information only to those Representatives who need to k now such information for the purpose of its business relationship with PHCS and who shall (i) be advised by the Recipient of this Agreement and the confidential and non -public nature of the Confidential Information, and (ii) agree with the Recipient to be bound by the provisions hereof. The Recipient shall only be responsible for any willful improper use of the Confidential Information by its Representatives and agrees, at its own expense, to take all reasonable measures, i ncluding but not limited to court proceedings, to restrain its Representatives from unauthorized disclosure or use of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the Recipient's responsibility for improper use of confidential information by its Representatives, limiting it to 'willful' improper use. The contradicted text includes the changed text and states that the recipient is responsible for willful improper use. The original text did not limit responsibility to willful actions, making the change a contradiction to the earlier definition of responsibility.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding anything to the contrary contained in Parag raph 1, Confidential Information does not include information which (i) was or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of a disclosure by the Recipient or its Representatives; or (ii) was or becomes available to the Recipient on a non -confidential basis from a source other than PHCS, provided that such source is not bound by a confidentiality agreement with PHCS or otherwise prohibited from transmitti ng the information to the Recipient; or (iii) was within the Recipient’s possession prior to its being furnished by or on behalf of PHCS, provided that the source of such information was not bound by a confidentiality agreement with PHCS in respect thereof or otherwise pr ohibited from transmitt ing the information to the Recipient.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding anything to the contrary contained in Parag raph 1, Confidential Information does not include information which (i) was or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of a disclosure by the Recipient; or (ii) was or becomes available to the Recipient on a non -confidential basis from a source other than PHCS, provided that such source is not bound by a confidentiality agreement with PHCS or otherwise prohibited from transmitti ng the information to the Recipient; or (iii) was within the Recipient’s possession prior to its being furnished by or on behalf of PHCS, provided that the source of such information was not bound by a confidentiality agreement with PHCS in respect thereof or otherwise pr ohibited from transmitt ing the information to the Recipient.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'or its Representatives' from exception (i), this change creates an in-text contradiction. Paragraph 3 states the Recipient is responsible for its Representatives. By removing Representatives from this exception, it is unclear whether disclosure by a Representative that makes the information public is still a breach of contract if it was not disclosed by the Recipient. Thus conflicting with paragraph 3, specifically the 'Recipient agrees to treat the Confidential Information which it receives as it would its own confidential information and to take all reasonable precautions to prevent the unauthorized disclosure to any third party of any Confidential Information.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: Use of Confidential Information . As a condition to the disclosure of the Confidential Information, the Recipient agrees to keep the Confidential Information confidential and not to disclose or otherwise use the Confi dential Information for any purpose, other than for its business relationship with PHCS, without the prior written consent of PHCS. Accordingly, the Recipient agrees to treat the C onfidential Information which it receives as it would its own confidential information and to take all reasonable precautions to prevent the unauthorized disclosure to any third party of any Confidential Information. The Recipient further agrees to disclose the Con fidential Information only to those Representatives who need to k now such information for the purpose of its business relationship with PHCS and who shall (i) be advised by the Recipient of this Agreement and the confidential and non -public nature of the Confidential Information, and (ii) agree with the Recipient to be bound by the provisions hereof. The Recipient shall be responsible for any improper use of the Confidential Information by its Representatives and agrees, at its own expense, to take all reasonable measures, i ncluding but not limited to court proceedings, to restrain its Representatives from unauthorized disclosure or use of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text states Confidential Information does not include information that becomes public via the Recipient 'or its Representatives'. The changed text removes 'or its Representatives' from this exception. The contradicted text states the Recipient is responsible for its Representatives' actions regarding confidential information. Therefore, the change creates an in-text contradiction because it's unclear whether a disclosure by a Representative that makes information public is still a breach of contract if not disclosed by the Recipient, directly conflicting with the Recipient's responsibility for its Representatives' actions.

perturbation.original_text: The Confide ntial Information disclosed shall at all times remain the property of PHCS. No license of any trade secrets, copyrights, patents or other rights is granted by this Agreement or by any disclosure of Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: The Confide ntial Information disclosed shall at all times remain the property of PHCS, except if the Recipient makes substantial improvements to it. No license of any trade secrets, copyrights, patents or other rights is granted by this Agreement or by any disclosure of Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: This change adds an exception that allows the Recipient to claim ownership of the Confidential Information if they make substantial improvements. This contradicts the original statement that the Confidential Information shall 'at all times remain the property of PHCS.' It creates an in-text contradiction as to who owns the improved information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: No License or Transfer . The Confide ntial Information disclosed shall at all times remain the property of PHCS, except if the Recipient makes substantial improvements to it. No license of any trade secrets, copyrights, patents or other rights is granted by this Agreement or by any disclosure of Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an exception to the original statement that 'Confidential Information disclosed shall at all times remain the property of PHCS.' The addition of 'except if the Recipient makes substantial improvements to it' creates a direct contradiction with the contradicted text, which also states that 'Confidential Information disclosed shall at all times remain the property of PHCS, except if the Recipient makes substantial improvements to it.' Both texts now conflict on the ownership of the confidential information when improvements are made. This aligns with the definition of Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction, as the contract now provides conflicting ownership stipulations in the changed and contradicted sections, creating a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Upon PHCS’ request, all Confidential Information (and all copies, extracts or other reproductions in whole or in part thereof), whether in writing or stored or maintained in or by electronic, magnetic or other means, media or devices, sh all be returned or destroyed (such destruction to be certified in writing) by an authorized officer and not retained in any form or for any reason.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon PHCS’ request, all Confidential Information (and all copies, extracts or other reproductions in whole or in part thereof), whether in writing or stored or maintained in or by electronic, magnetic or other means, media or devices, sh all be returned or destroyed (such destruction to be certified in writing) by an authorized officer and not retained in any form or for any reason, except for archival purposes.
perturbation.explanation: This change adds 'except for archival purposes' to the end of the clause regarding the return/destruction of confidential information. This directly contradicts the requirement that the confidential information is 'not retained in any form or for any reason'. It is unclear whether PHCS can demand destruction of all archival copies, causing an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Destruction/Return of Confiden tial Information . Upon PHCS’ request, all Confidential Information (and all copies, extracts or other reproductions in whole or in part thereof), whether in writing or stored or maintained in or by electronic, magnetic or other means, media or devices, sh all be returned or destroyed (such destruction to be certified in writing) by an authorized officer and not retained in any form or for any reason, except for archival purposes. Confidentiality and Non -Disclosure Agreement Page 2 of 2
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds 'except for archival purposes' to the original text regarding the return/destruction of confidential information. The original text stated the information must be 'not retained in any form or for any reason.' The addition of the exception directly contradicts the original stipulation that there should be no retention, causing an in-text contradiction. The Contradicted Text is exactly the same as the Changed Text, confirming that the change introduces a contradiction within the text itself, as defined by Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement represents the entire agreement among the parties relating to the treatment of Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement represents the entire agreement among the parties relating to the treatment of Confidential Information, unless superseded by a subsequent written agreement concerning a specific project.
perturbation.explanation: By adding 'unless superseded by a subsequent written agreement concerning a specific project,' this change creates an in-text contradiction. While claiming to be the entire agreement it now allows for exceptions which undermines this declaration and it is no longer clear if the agreement is final. This would open up disputes on other documents overriding the confidentiality agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Miscellaneous . It is unders tood and agreed that no failure or delay by PHCS in exercising any right, power or privilege hereunder shall operate as a waiver thereof nor shall any single or partial exercise thereof preclude any other or further exercise of any ri ght, power or privileg e hereunder. This Agreement (i) shall not be assigned by any party without the prior written consent of the other party; (ii) cannot be amended, nor can any of its provisions be waived, except by a writing signed by the parties; and (iii) shall be binding on and inure to the benefit of the parties and their respective successors and assigns. This Agreement shall be governed and co nstrued in accordance with the laws of the State of New Jersey without giving effect to the conflict of laws provisions thereof . This Agreement represents the entire agreement among the parties relating to the treatment of Confidential Information, unless superseded by a subsequent written agreement concerning a specific project. IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the parties hereto have executed this Agreement under their respective hands as of the day and year first above written. RECIPIENT Signature: By Name (print): Title: Date: PRINCETON HEALTHCARE SYSTEM By: Name: Title: Date:
perturbation.justification: The original text states the agreement represents the *entire* agreement regarding confidential information. The changed text adds an exception: 'unless superseded by a subsequent written agreement concerning a specific project.' This directly contradicts the claim of being the *entire* agreement. The contradicted text also states the agreement represents the entire agreement, with the added caveat that it can't be amended except in writing signed by the parties. The change introduces a loophole where subsequent agreements can override the original confidentiality agreement, even if they don't meet the amendment requirements, thereby creating a strong in-text contradiction.
